title: Decline and Collapse of the Ottoman Empire
summary: How an empire that spanned three continents slowly unravelled - hollowed out by inflation and corruption, halted at Vienna in 1683, outpaced by Russia and Europe, fractured by nationalism, and finally swept away by World War I and the birth of the Turkish Republic.

context.headline: The slow unravelling of an empire
context.description: For nearly three centuries the Ottoman Empire shrank from a feared superpower into the 'sick man of Europe.' Its retreat began at the gates of Vienna, accelerated under Russian and nationalist pressure, and ended in the ashes of World War I - replaced by a new Turkish republic with its capital at Ankara.
